event: The Death (Dormition) of the Virgin Mary
symbols: Mary on her deathbed — the end of the earthly life of the Mother of God
apostles gathered around — the whole Church mourning and honoring Mary
candles and liturgical objects — the funeral rites of the faithful
architectural setting with church — the ecclesial context of Mary's passing
description: A fresco showing a wide scene of the death of the Virgin Mary. At the center, Mary lies on a decorated bier surrounded by a large crowd of apostles, bishops, and mourners in various colored robes. Some kneel, some stand, some gesture in grief. The scene is set in an architectural space with buildings and a church bell tower visible in the background. Hills and a landscape with a distant city are visible beyond. In the upper portion, a small heavenly vision appears. The composition is horizontal and crowded with figures, characteristic of early Renaissance narrative fresco painting.
